stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • The Akashi Kaikyō Bridge is a suspension bridge in Japan.
  • It connects the city of Kobe to Awaji Island.
  • Its main span is roughly 1.24 miles long.
  • The Great Belt Bridge is a suspension bridge in Denmark.
  • It connects the islands of Zealand and Funen.
  • Its main span is about 1.03 miles long.
stem: The student wants to compare the main span lengths of the two suspension bridges. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: The Akashi Kaikyō Bridge, whose main span is roughly 1.24 miles long, connects the city of Kobe to Awaji Island.
Both the Akashi Kaikyō Bridge, which is located in Japan, and the Great Belt Bridge, which is located in Denmark, are examples of suspension bridges.
Some of the world's suspension bridges, including one bridge between Zealand and Funen in Denmark, have spans longer than a mile.
The main span of the Akashi Kaikyō Bridge is roughly 1.24 miles long, while the main span of the slightly shorter Great Belt Bridge is about 1.03 miles long.

rationale: Choice D is the best answer because it compares the lengths of the main spans of the two named bridges, stating both figures. Choice A is incorrect because it describes only one bridge. Choice B is incorrect because it identifies both bridges by type and location without comparing their span lengths. Choice C is incorrect because it makes a general statement about suspension bridges that does not compare the two specific bridges.
